name: ExtensionObservation_Instantiates
title: R5: Instantiates FHIR ObservationDefinition (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Observation.instantiates[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Observation.instantiates[x]` 0..1 `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ObservationDefinition), Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ObservationDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Observation.instantiates[x]` 0..1 `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ObservationDefinition), Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ObservationDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Observation.instantiates[x]` has a context of Observation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Observation`.
Element `Observation.instantiates[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `Observation.instantiates[x]` has a context of Observation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Observation`.
Element `Observation.instantiates[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
ObservationDefinition can be referenced by its canonical url using instantiatesCanonical, or by a name or an identifier using the appropriate sub-elements of instantiatesReference.

element.definition: The reference to a FHIR ObservationDefinition resource that provides the definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this Observation instance.
element.comment: ObservationDefinition can be referenced by its canonical url using instantiatesCanonical, or by a name or an identifier using the appropriate sub-elements of instantiatesReference.
